title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: document provides guidance AI agents working RWisecondorX.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Build R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), comparable scope NIPTUtils built entirely Rduckhts/DuckDB rather Rsamtools/Python. package ports two upstream implementations: WisecondorX — copy number variation detection cfDNA (Python/pysam upstream) NIPTeR — fast trisomy prediction via chromosomal fraction Z-scores, NCV scores, regression, chi-squared correction (R/Rsamtools upstream) Design priorities: Exact conformance upstream implementations documented testable. Performance — DuckDB SQL replaces per-read Python loops Rsamtools scans; large BAMs run seconds, minutes. Interoperable file formats — bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED files intermediate layer binning analysis, consumable DuckDB, R, Python, tabix-aware tool. Python runtime dependency — Rduckhts replaces pysam HTS operations; reticulate condathis optional used conformance testing.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Runtime R package functionality avoids requiring Python default. condathis optional used reproducible conformance test environments; installs official wisecondorx package bioconda, vendored copy. vendored Python implementation removed inst/python/wisecondorx/ avoid licence drift maintenance burden. canonical upstream reference ../../duckhts/.sync/WisecondorX/ (algorithm reference ) official bioconda package (execution reference). NIPTeR upstream reference ../../duckhts/.sync/NIPTeR/ (read-algorithm reference). reticulate remains optional dependency used conformance tests optional helpers.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: R/convert.R — shared BAM/CRAM binning engine (bam_convert(), bam_convert_bed()); central read-counting layer used WisecondorX NIPTeR layers. R/nipter_bin.R — NIPTeR binning layer (nipter_bin_bam(), nipter_bin_bam_bed()); produces NIPTeRSample objects bgzipped BED output. R/nipter_gc.R (planned) — nipter_gc_correct() using rduckhts_fasta_nuc() GC content; bundled correction tables. R/nipter_score.R (planned) — nipter_z_score(), nipter_ncv_score(), nipter_regression(). R/nipter_chi.R (planned) — nipter_chi_correct(). R/nipter_control.R (planned) — nipter_as_control_group() related group management. R/wisecondorx_cli.R — CLI wrappers (wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict()). R/npz.R — bam_convert_npz() WisecondorX-compatible NPZ output. R/aaa.R — SRA metadata helpers. inst/tinytest/ — unit tests (one file per feature family). inst/extdata/ — synthetic BAM/CRAM fixtures generated scripts/make_fixtures.sh. inst/python/ directory — vendored Python removed. WisecondorX upstream algorithm reference ../../duckhts/.sync/WisecondorX/. NIPTeR upstream algorithm reference ../../duckhts/.sync/NIPTeR/. WisecondorX conformance script ../../duckhts/scripts/wisecondorx_convert_conformance.py.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: BED.gz format language-agnostic handoff binning downstream analysis: Four-column WisecondorX BED: chrom, start, end, count (written bam_convert_bed()). Five-column NIPTeR BED: chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()). Coordinates 0-based half-open intervals (BED convention). Chromosomes use chr prefix. files bgzipped (BGZF) tabix-indexed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(). use gzfile() external tools. files queryable directly DuckDB using read_csv() hts_read_bgzf_bed() functions.

what: Convert Step: Exact SQL Replication of pysam larp/larp2
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: WisecondorX convert step uses streaming dedup state machine (larp/larp2). exact SQL equivalent uses DuckDB window functions FILE_OFFSET (column added read_bam() duckhts extension captures BGZF virtual offset read, giving monotonic BAM file order): Key subtleties reproduced exactly: Improper pairs (is_paired is_proper) invisible larp larp2. filter applied rmdup = "streaming" — intrinsic WisecondorX algorithm, user flag option. Unpaired reads update larp larp2 — hence IGNORE NULLS trick. larp never reset chromosomes — hence PARTITION . Bin assignment uses integer division pos // binsize (/), matching Python’s int(pos / binsize). pos 0-based: subtract 1 duckhts 1-based POS dividing. conformance script achieves exact bin--bin match HG00106.chrom11 (25,115 non-zero bins, 0 mismatches).
code: LAG(pos) OVER (ORDER BY file_offset)                                          -- prev_pos (larp) LAST_VALUE(CASE WHEN is_paired != 0 THEN pnext END IGNORE NULLS)              -- prev_pnext (larp2)     OVER (ORDER BY file_offset ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: bam_convert() bam_convert_bed() expose samtools-style -f/-F flag filtering: require_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & require_flags) == require_flags kept. Equivalent samtools view -f. exclude_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. Equivalent samtools view -F. Use duckhts UDFs sam_flag_bits(flag) sam_flag_has(flag, bit) inspect SAM flags SQL needed. NIPTeR layer (nipter_bin_bam()) uses require_flags = 0L, exclude_flags = 0L default (mapped reads, matching NIPTeR’s scanBam defaults).

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR port follows rewrites.bio principles WisecondorX port: exact emulation, credit original authors, AI transparency. Key differences original: Rsamtools::scanBam() replaced bam_convert() (DuckDB/Rduckhts SQL engine). GC content derived FASTA reference via rduckhts_fasta_nuc() — bundled correction tables. NIPTeRSample objects use list structure NIPTeR’s NIPTSample (autosomal_chromosome_reads, sex_chromosome_reads, correction status fields, sample_name). Strand separation (separate_strands = TRUE) stubbed informative error; requires forward/reverse SQL split added regression layer.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Always read existing implementation changing : Read relevant R files, package metadata, tests proposing changes. upstream WisecondorX behavior unclear, consult ../../duckhts/.sync/WisecondorX/ (algorithm reference ). look inst/python/ — removed. upstream NIPTeR behavior unclear, consult ../../duckhts/.sync/NIPTeR/. conformance testing, use official wisecondorx bioconda package via condathis, vendored copy. Preserve CRAN-friendly package behavior. make package loading depend preconfigured external Python installation. Keep package runtime logic conformance tooling separate. Keep WisecondorX NIPTeR layers separate files strict naming conventions (nipter_*.R vs. wisecondorx_*.R). shared engine convert.R.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: README.Rmd primary editable README source. Run make readme regenerate README.md. Keep package metadata DESCRIPTION aligned actual runtime strategy. R documentation NAMESPACE generated roxygen2 tags. Run make rd changing @export, @param, @seealso tags. manually edit generated artifacts source file exists upstream .

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: hand-edit NAMESPACE; regenerate make rd. hand-edit README.md; edit README.Rmd run make readme. Treat generated files outputs package workflow, primary editing targets.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Prefer repository Makefile ad hoc command sequences appropriate target exists. Use make targets: make rd (roxygen2), make test (tinytest), make readme (render README), make fixtures (synthetic BAMs). Keep build steps deterministic non-interactive. Keep CRAN-facing package behavior intact changing build test workflows.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: make package loading depend reticulate Python availability. Use reticulate primarily tests, conformance helpers, explicitly optional workflows. optional Python-backed helpers added, guard requireNamespace("reticulate", quietly = TRUE). Avoid hard-coding machine-local Python paths package code.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Conformance tests optional skip cleanly condathis reticulate unavailable. Install official wisecondorx bioconda package via condathis reproducible reference runs. vendor Python source. rmdup parameter bam_convert() must support: "streaming" (WisecondorX larp/larp2), "none" (NIPTeR default / --normdup), "flag" (Picard/sambamba pre-marked BAMs).

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Use tinytest default R package test framework. Add package tests inst/tinytest/, preferably one file per feature family workflow. R-facing behavior changes, update add tinytest coverage rather relying manual checks. Tests depend Python reticulate must skip cleanly dependencies unavailable.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR authors Dirk de Weerd Lennart Johansson listed cph DESCRIPTION accordance rewrites.bio attribution principle. WisecondorX authors listed ctb. new upstream algorithm ported must original authors credited DESCRIPTION appropriate role (cph copyright holders ported code).

title: Background
text: extensively comparing different (shallow) whole-genome sequencing-based copy number detection tools, including WISECONDOR, QDNAseq, CNVkit, Control-FREEC, BIC-seq2 cn.MOPS, WISECONDOR appeared normalize sequencing data consistent way, shown paper. Nevertheless, WISECONDOR limitations: Stouffer’s Z-score approach error-prone dealing large amounts aberrations, algorithm extremely slow (24h) operating small bin sizes (15 kb), sex chromosomes part analysis. , present WisecondorX, evolved WISECONDOR aims dealing previous difficulties, resulting overall superior results significantly lower computing times, allowing daily diagnostic use. WisecondorX meant applicable NIPT, also gDNA, PGT, FFPE, LQB, … etc.

title: Background
text: found superior results WisecondorX using bowtie2 mapper. Note important read quality filtering executed prior running WisecondorX: software requires low-quality reads distinguish informative bins non-informative ones.

title: Background
text: Stable releases can installed pip install. option ascertains latest version downloaded, however, install R dependencies. Alternatively, Conda additionally installs necessary depedencies, however, latest version might downloaded.
code: pip install -U git+https://github.com/CenterForMedicalGeneticsGhent/WisecondorX conda install -f -c conda-forge -c bioconda wisecondorx

title: Background
text: three main stages (converting, reference creating predicting) using WisecondorX: Convert aligned reads .npz files (reference test samples) Automated gender prediction, required consistently analyze sex chromosomes, based Gaussian mixture model. samples (<20) included reference creation, male female samples (NIPT, means male female feti) represented, process might accurate. Therefore, alternatively, one can manually tweak --yfrac parameter. paramount importance reference set consists exclusively negative control samples originate sequencer, mapper, reference genome, type material, … etc, test samples. rule thumb, think laboratory silico steps: sources bias can omitted, better. Try include least 50 samples per reference. better, yet, 500 unlikely observe additional improvement concerning normalization. Predict copy number alterations (using reference file test .npz cases interest)

title: Background
text: → Bash recipe docs/include/pipeline/convert.sh
code: WisecondorX convert input.bam/cram output.npz [--optional arguments]

title: Background
text: → Bash recipe docs/include/pipeline/newref.sh
code: WisecondorX newref reference_input_dir/*.npz reference_output.npz [--optional arguments]

title: Background
text: (*) least one output formats selected → Bash recipe docs/include/pipeline/predict.sh
code: WisecondorX predict test_input.npz reference_input.npz output_id [--optional arguments]

title: Background
text: Returns gender according reference.
code: WisecondorX gender test_input.npz reference_input.npz

title: Background
text: default parameters optimized shallow whole-genome sequencing data (0.1x - 1x coverage) reference bin sizes ranging 50 500 kb.

title: Background
text: understand underlying algorithm, highly recommend reading Straver et al (2014); update shortly introduced Huijsdens-van Amsterdam et al (2018). Numerous adaptations algorithm made, yet central normalization principles remain. Changes include e.g. inclusion gender prediction algorithm, gender handling prior normalization (ultimately enabling X Y predictions), -sample Z-scoring, inclusion weighted circular binary segmentation algorithm improved codes obtaining tables plots.

title: Background
text: Every dot represents bin. dots range across X-axis chromosome 1 X (Y, case male). vertical position dot represents ratio observed number reads expected number reads, latter ‘normal’ state. values log2-transformed, copy neutral dots close-0. Importantly, notice dots always subject Gaussian noise. Therefore, segments, indicated horizontal white lines, cover bins predicted equal copy number. size dots represents variability reference set. Thus, size increases certainty observation. goes line width segments. Vertical grey bars represent blacklist, matches mostly hypervariable loci repeats. Finally, horizontal colored dotted lines show constitutional 1n 3n states expected (constitutional DNA 100% purity). Often, aberration reach limits, several potential causes: depending type analysis, sample subject tumor fraction, fetal fraction, mosaicism, … etc. Sometimes, segments surpass limits: ’s likely dealing 0n, 4n, 5n, 6n, …

title: Background
text: file contains bin-wise information. data ‘NaN’, corresponding bin included blacklist. Z-scores calculated default using within-sample reference bins null set.

title: Background
text: file contains segment-wise information. combined Z-score calculated using -sample Z-scoring technique (test case vs reference cases).

title: Background
text: file contains aberrant segments, defined --beta --zscore parameters.

title: Background
text: file contains interesting statistics (per chromosome overall). definition Z-scores matches one ‘ID_segments.bed’. Particularly interesting NIPT.

title: Background
text: jsonlite (v1.8.8) DNAcopy (v1.76.0) scipy (v1.13.0) scikit-learn (v1.4.2) pysam (v0.22.0) numpy (v1.26.4) matplotlib (v3.8.4) pandas (2.2.2) course, versions likely work well.

what: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX R port WisecondorX built top Rduckhts DuckDB. follows rewrites.bio principles: exact emulation upstream outputs, full credit original authors, transparency AI assistance. core convert step runs entirely R/SQL via Rduckhts Python dependency. bam_convert() returns per-bin read counts memory; bam_convert_bed() writes bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED file readable DuckDB tabix-aware tool; bam_convert_npz() serialises WisecondorX-compatible .npz via reticulate. convert implementation exactly replicates upstream larp / larp2 streaming deduplication behaviour, achieving bin--bin agreement Python implementation. pipelines need full upstream toolchain, thin condathis wrappers cover every stage: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() delegate official bioconda package without requiring manual conda setup.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: Install development version GitHub: RWisecondorX imports Rduckhts, DBI, duckdb. reticulate needed write .npz files; condathis needed upstream CLI wrappers. Neither required core convert step.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("sounkou-bioinfo/RWisecondorX")

what: Convert A BAM To Bin Counts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: package ships small chromosome 11 BAM fixture examples tests. rmdup argument controls duplicate handling. "streaming" exactly replicates upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 state machine default. "none" skips deduplication entirely, matching wisecondorx convert --normdup NIPT data read depth low. "flag" uses SAM duplicate flag (0x400) BAMs already processed Picard sambamba.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  fixture_bam <- system.file(   "extdata",   "hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam",   package = "RWisecondorX" )  bins <- bam_convert(   fixture_bam,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup = "streaming" )  names(Filter(Negate(is.null), bins)) #> [1] "11"  length(bins[["11"]]) #> [1] 1198  sum(bins[["11"]]) #> [1] 982

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: bam_convert_bed() language-agnostic output path. produces bgzipped BED file (chrom, start, end, count; 0-based coordinates) tabix index alongside , using rduckhts_bgzip rduckhts_tabix_index Rduckhts — external tools Python required. resulting file can queried region directly DuckDB.
code: bam_convert_bed(   bam     = "sample.bam",   bed     = "sample.bed.gz",   # → sample.bed.gz + sample.bed.gz.tbi   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup   = "streaming" )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: full upstream WisecondorX pipeline needed, wisecondorx_convert() wraps wisecondorx convert via condathis produces .npz official Python implementation. wisecondorx_newref() wisecondorx_predict() cover remaining stages. three handle conda environment creation automatically first use. CLI wrappers expose upstream arguments documented WisecondorX README. delegate official wisecondorx bioconda package installed automatically via condathis.
code: wisecondorx_convert(   bam     = "sample.bam",   npz     = "sample.npz",   binsize = 5000L,   normdup = FALSE          # TRUE → --normdup (NIPT) ) library(RWisecondorX)  fixture_npz <- tempfile(fileext = ".npz") np <- reticulate::import("numpy", convert = FALSE)  bam_convert_npz(   bam     = fixture_bam,   npz     = fixture_npz,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup   = "streaming",   np      = np )  c(   npz_created = file.exists(fixture_npz),   npz_size    = file.info(fixture_npz)$size ) #> npz_created    npz_size  #>           1         392 library(RWisecondorX)  reference_npz <- tempfile(fileext = ".npz") plotyfrac_png <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") output_prefix <- tempfile(pattern = "wisecondorx-")  wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files   = c(fixture_npz, fixture_npz),   output      = reference_npz,   binsize     = 5000L,   ref_binsize = 50000L,   nipt        = TRUE,   refsize     = 300L,   yfrac       = NULL,   plotyfrac   = plotyfrac_png,   cpus        = 1L )  wisecondorx_predict(   npz          = fixture_npz,   ref          = reference_npz,   output_prefix = output_prefix,   ref_binsize  = 50000L,   minrefbins   = 150L,   maskrepeats  = 5L,   zscore       = 5,   alpha        = 1e-4,   beta         = NULL,   blacklist    = NULL,   gender       = NULL,   bed          = TRUE,   plot         = FALSE,   regions      = NULL,   ylim         = NULL,   cairo        = FALSE,   seed         = 1L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: sra_runinfo_url(), download_sra_runinfo(), read_sra_runinfo() small helpers staging NCBI SRA run metadata used conformance fixtures.
code: library(RWisecondorX)  sra_runinfo_url("PRJNA400134") #> [1] "https://trace.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Traces/sra-db-be/runinfo?acc=PRJNA400134"

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: README.Rmd editable source document. Run make readme rerender README.md, make rd regenerate documentation roxygen2 comments, make fixtures rebuild bundled test data.

what: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Core read-counting engine shared WisecondorX NIPTeR layers. Reads aligned reads BAM CRAM file returns per-bin read counts chromosomes 1-22 sex chromosomes (X mapped key "23", Y "24").

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: bam_convert(   bam,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 5000L (WisecondorX); use 50000L NIPTeR-style workflows. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 1L (WisecondorX / samtools default). Set 0L retain reads regardless MAPQ (NIPTeR). require_flags Integer bitmask. reads (FLAG & require_flags) == require_flags retained. 0L (default) imposes requirement. Example: require_flags = 0x2L keeps properly paired reads. exclude_flags Integer bitmask. Reads (FLAG & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. 0L (default) drops nothing. Example: exclude_flags = 0xF04L drops unmapped, secondary, QC-fail supplementary reads (common samtools pre-filter). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "streaming" (default) applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 algorithm (also excludes improper pairs). "flag" drops reads SAM flag 0x400. "none" keeps reads pass filters. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default) temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: named list one integer vector per chromosome key ("1"– "22", "23" X, "24" Y). vector contains per-bin read counts (bin 0 = positions 0 binsize - 1). Chromosomes absent BAM NULL.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Read filtering mirrors samtools view convention: mapq sets minimum mapping quality; require_flags bitmask flags must set (equivalent samtools view -f); exclude_flags bitmask flags must clear (equivalent samtools view -F).  Use duckhts UDF sam_flag_bits(flag) inspect named flag fields, sam_flag_has(flag, bit) test individual bits. rmdup controls duplicate removal independently flag filters: "streaming" applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 consecutive-position state machine also enforces WisecondorX improper-pair rule (paired reads properly paired excluded counting dedup state — intrinsic algorithm, flag option); "flag" additionally excludes reads SAM flag 0x400 set (Picard / sambamba pre-marked duplicates); "none" applies deduplication.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # WisecondorX defaults bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam")  # NIPTeR defaults — all mapped reads, no dedup, 50 kb bins bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L, mapq = 0L,                     rmdup = "none")  # Pre-filtered BAM: skip unmapped + secondary + supplementary, flag dedup bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam",                     exclude_flags = bitwOr(0x4L, bitwOr(0x100L, 0x800L)),                     rmdup = "flag") } # }

what: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: Runs bam_convert() writes per-bin read counts bgzipped, tab-delimited BED file (four columns: chrom, start, end, count), creates tabix index (.tbi) alongside via Rduckhts. language-agnostic intermediate format RWisecondorX native pipeline; file can queried directly duckhts/DuckDB tabix-aware tool.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
code: bam_convert_bed(   bam,   bed,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "none", "flag"),   con = NULL,   reference = NULL,   index = TRUE )

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file (created overwritten). tabix index written paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 1L). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L (nothing dropped). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default), temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; TRUE (default) tabix index created alongside bgzipped output.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bed (invisibly).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: Coordinates 0-based half-open intervals matching BED convention (start = bin_index * binsize, end = start + binsize). Chromosomes written 1–22, X, Y (chr prefix) numeric order. bins written, including count zero. bgzip tabix indexing performed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(), external tools required.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bam_convert_bed("sample.bam", "sample.bed.gz", binsize = 5000, rmdup = "streaming") } # }

what: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: Runs bam_convert() serialises resulting bin-count list .npz file byte-compatible file written wisecondorx convert.  NPZ must created numpy wisecondorx newref can load ; function therefore requires reticulate Python environment numpy installed (version ≥ 1.16 works — format stable).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
code: bam_convert_npz(   bam,   npz,   binsize = 5000L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "none", "flag"),   con = NULL,   np = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. npz Path output .npz file (created overwritten). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. Passed bam_convert(). np Optional numpy module imported via reticulate::import("numpy"). NULL (default) imported automatically. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. Passed bam_convert().

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: npz (invisibly).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bam_convert_npz("sample.bam", "sample.npz", binsize = 5000, rmdup = "streaming") } # }

what: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: Replicates NIPTeR's bin_bam_sample(): counts reads fixed-width bins across chromosomes 1-22, X, Y, MAPQ filter pair-status filter (mapped reads counted), matching Rsamtools scanBam defaults used original.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: nipter_bin_bam(   bam,   binsize = 50000L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 50000 (NIPTeR's fixed bin size). Must match binsize used building control group. rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "none" (default, NIPTeR standard) "flag" (BAMs already processed Picard / sambamba). NIPTeR perform streaming deduplication. separate_strands yet implemented. Set FALSE (default). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: object class NIPTeRSample: named list autosomal_chromosome_reads (list one 22-row integer matrix, rows named "1"–"22", columns bins), sex_chromosome_reads (list one 2-row integer matrix, rows named "X" "Y"), correction_status_autosomal ("Uncorrected"), correction_status_sex ("Uncorrected"), sample_name.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: Internally calls bam_convert() mapq = 0L, filter_improper_pairs = FALSE, caller-supplied rmdup. result reshaped NIPTeRSample object whose structure parallels NIPTeR's NIPTSample: autosomal reads chromosome--bin matrix sex chromosome reads separate two-row matrix. Strand separation (separate_strands = TRUE) yet implemented; requires forward/reverse split SQL layer added NIPTeR regression layer ported.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sample <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L) sample$autosomal_chromosome_reads[[1]]["21", ]   # chr21 bin counts } # }

what: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
text: Bins BAM/CRAM file NIPTeR defaults writes result bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED file five columns: chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count.

title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
code: nipter_bin_bam_bed(   bam,   bed,   binsize = 50000L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   corrected = NULL,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL,   index = TRUE )

title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file. tabix index written alongside paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 50000). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy: "none" (default) "flag". corrected Optional NIPTeRSample already processed nipter_gc_correct(). supplied, corrected counts populate corrected_count column; otherwise column NA. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; create tabix index (default TRUE).

title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
text: bed (invisibly).

title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
text: corrected_count filled NA nipter_gc_correct() available. GC correction ported, typical workflow : nipter_bin_bam() → nipter_gc_correct() → nipter_bin_bam_bed() corrected sample passed corrected.

title: Write NIPTeR-style bin counts to a bgzipped BED file — nipter_bin_bam_bed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ nipter_bin_bam_bed("sample.bam", "sample.nipter.bed.gz") } # }

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
text: helpers standardize RWisecondorX stages SRA run metadata conformance fixtures. use NCBI SRA backend runinfo endpoint default writing project metadata inst/extdata/ source tree.

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
code: sra_runinfo_url(accession, mode = c("acc", "term"))  download_sra_runinfo(   accession,   dest = file.path("inst", "extdata", paste0(accession, "_runinfo.csv")),   overwrite = FALSE,   quiet = TRUE,   mode = c("acc", "term"),   downloader = utils::download.file )  read_sra_runinfo(   accession,   path = system.file("extdata", paste0(accession, "_runinfo.csv"), package =     "RWisecondorX") )

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
text: accession study project accession PRJNA400134. mode Retrieval mode. "acc" queries backend acc=<accession>; "term" queries term=<accession>. dest Output CSV path. Defaults inst/extdata/<accession>_runinfo.csv current source checkout. overwrite Logical; overwrite existing file. quiet Logical; passed downloader. downloader Function used retrieve URL. Defaults utils::download.file(). injectable tests can avoid network calls. path Path run metadata CSV. Defaults bundled file inst/extdata/<accession>_runinfo.csv.

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
text: sra_runinfo_url() returns length-1 character URL. download_sra_runinfo() returns output path, invisibly. read_sra_runinfo() returns data.frame.

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
text: SRA Run Selector backend runinfo endpoints can expose slightly different columns. reproducible package fixtures, RWisecondorX standardizes backend runinfo CSV output.

title: SRA Run Metadata Utilities — sra_runinfo
code: sra_runinfo_url("PRJNA400134") #> [1] "https://trace.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Traces/sra-db-be/runinfo?acc=PRJNA400134"  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ download_sra_runinfo("PRJNA400134") metadata <- read_sra_runinfo("PRJNA400134") } # }

what: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
text: Calls wisecondorx convert via condathis::run() convert aligned BAM CRAM file .npz file downstream use wisecondorx_newref() wisecondorx_predict().

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
code: wisecondorx_convert(   bam,   npz,   reference = NULL,   binsize = 5000L,   normdup = FALSE,   env_name = "wisecondorx",   extra_args = character(0) )

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. npz Path output .npz file (created overwritten). reference Optional path FASTA reference file. Required bam CRAM file. Passed upstream --reference. binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). reference bin size multiple value. Passed upstream --binsize. normdup Logical; TRUE, passes --normdup skip duplicate removal. Recommended NIPT data read depth low. env_name Name conda environment containing wisecondorx (default "wisecondorx"). Created automatically condathis first use via bioconda channel. extra_args Character vector additional arguments passed verbatim mapped CLI flags. forward compatibility future upstream WisecondorX releases.

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
text: npz (invisibly).

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
text: wrapper exposes upstream wisecondorx convert CLI flags: --reference, --binsize, --normdup. fully native R implementation (Python dependency) uses Rduckhts instead pysam, see bam_convert() bam_convert_npz().

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format (upstream CLI wrapper) — wisecondorx_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wisecondorx_convert(   bam = "sample.bam",   npz = "sample.npz",   binsize = 5000L,   normdup = FALSE ) } # }

what: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
text: Calls wisecondorx newref via condathis::run() set NPZ files produced bam_convert_npz(). NPZ files must use binsize; reference built ref_binsize (must multiple binsize).

title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
code: wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files,   output,   binsize = 5000L,   ref_binsize = 50000L,   nipt = FALSE,   refsize = 300L,   yfrac = NULL,   plotyfrac = NULL,   cpus = 1L,   env_name = "wisecondorx",   extra_args = character(0) )

title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
text: npz_files Character vector paths .npz files (one per sample). output Path output reference .npz file. binsize Convert-step bin size base pairs (default 5000). Must match binsize used calling bam_convert_npz(). ref_binsize Reference bin size base pairs (default 50000). Passed upstream --binsize. Must multiple binsize. nipt Logical; pass upstream --nipt. refsize Number reference locations per target bin. Passed upstream --refsize. yfrac Optional numeric Y-read fraction cutoff. Passed upstream --yfrac. plotyfrac Optional output path Y-fraction histogram mixture-model plot. Passed upstream --plotyfrac. cpus Number CPUs pass wisecondorx newref (default 1). env_name Name conda environment containing wisecondorx (default "wisecondorx"). Created automatically condathis first use via bioconda channel. extra_args Character vector additional arguments passed verbatim mapped CLI flags. Keep forward compatibility future upstream WisecondorX releases.

title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
text: output (invisibly).

title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
text: wrapper exposes current upstream wisecondorx newref CLI flags documented WisecondorX README: --nipt, --binsize, --refsize, --yfrac, --plotyfrac, --cpus.

title: Build a WisecondorX reference panel — wisecondorx_newref
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wisecondorx_newref(   npz_files = list.files("controls/", "\\.npz$", full.names = TRUE),   output = "reference.npz",   binsize = 5000L,   ref_binsize = 50000L,   nipt = TRUE,   refsize = 300L,   yfrac = 0.05,   cpus = 1L ) } # }

what: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
text: Calls wisecondorx predict via condathis::run() single-sample NPZ file reference panel built wisecondorx_newref().

title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
code: wisecondorx_predict(   npz,   ref,   output_prefix,   ref_binsize = 50000L,   minrefbins = 150L,   maskrepeats = 5L,   zscore = 5,   alpha = 1e-04,   beta = NULL,   blacklist = NULL,   gender = NULL,   bed = FALSE,   plot = FALSE,   regions = NULL,   ylim = NULL,   cairo = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   env_name = "wisecondorx",   extra_args = character(0) )

title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
text: npz Path sample .npz file (bam_convert_npz()). ref Path reference .npz file (wisecondorx_newref()). output_prefix Output prefix wisecondorx predict output files (e.g. "results/sample1"). ref_binsize Reference bin size base pairs (default 50000). Passed upstream --binsize. Must match ref_binsize used building reference. minrefbins Minimum number sensible reference bins per target bin. Passed upstream --minrefbins. maskrepeats Number repeat-masking cycles. Passed upstream --maskrepeats. zscore Z-score threshold aberration calling. Passed upstream --zscore. alpha P-value cutoff circular binary segmentation breakpoints. Passed upstream --alpha. beta Optional ratio cutoff aberration calling. Passed upstream --beta. set, upstream ignores zscore. blacklist Optional path headerless BED blacklist file. Passed upstream --blacklist. gender Optional forced gender, "F" "M". Passed upstream --gender. bed Logical; also write BED output files. Passed upstream --bed. plot Logical; also write plot output files. Passed upstream --plot. regions Optional path headerless BED file regions mark plot. Passed upstream --regions. ylim Optional numeric vector length 2 giving y-axis interval, e.g. c(-2, 2). Passed upstream --ylim [,b]. cairo Logical; use Cairo bitmap output. Passed upstream --cairo. seed Optional integer random seed segmentation. Passed upstream --seed. env_name Name conda environment containing wisecondorx (default "wisecondorx"). extra_args Character vector additional arguments passed verbatim mapped CLI flags. Keep forward compatibility future upstream WisecondorX releases.

title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
text: output_prefix (invisibly).

title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
text: wrapper exposes current upstream wisecondorx predict CLI flags documented WisecondorX README: --minrefbins, --maskrepeats, --zscore, --alpha, --beta, --blacklist, --gender, --bed, --plot, --regions, --ylim, --cairo, --seed.

title: Predict copy-number aberrations with WisecondorX — wisecondorx_predict
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ wisecondorx_predict(   npz = "sample.npz",   ref = "reference.npz",   output_prefix = "results/sample",   ref_binsize = 50000L,   minrefbins = 150L,   maskrepeats = 5L,   zscore = 5,   alpha = 1e-4,   beta = NULL,   blacklist = NULL,   gender = "F",   bed = TRUE,   plot = FALSE,   regions = NULL,   ylim = c(-2, 2),   cairo = FALSE,   seed = 1L ) } # }
